terms.etiology_clue: Autosomal dominant APC gene mutation causing multiple osteomas, supernumerary teeth, and colorectal polyposis with malignant potential.
terms.management_clue: Colorectal surveillance and polypectomy are essential; jaw osteomas treated by surgical excision if symptomatic.

terms.etiology_clue: Pseudocyst of unknown etiology containing empty or fluid-filled cavity with no epithelial lining, typically in young patients.
terms.management_clue: Surgical exploration and curettage to stimulate bleeding and bony healing; excellent prognosis with low recurrence.

terms.etiology_clue: Benign intraosseous lesion of unknown etiology characterised by multinucleated giant cells within a fibrovascular stroma.
terms.management_clue: Curettage for non-aggressive lesions; intralesional corticosteroids or calcitonin as alternatives; resection reserved for aggressive recurrent cases.

terms.etiology_clue: Malignant neoplasm of glandular epithelial origin arising from minor salivary glands or metastatic deposits within the jaws.
terms.management_clue: Surgical resection with clear margins combined with radiotherapy and chemotherapy depending on stage and site of origin.

terms.etiology_clue: Developmental odontogenic cyst formed by fluid accumulation between reduced enamel epithelium and the crown of an unerupted tooth.
terms.management_clue: Enucleation with extraction of associated impacted tooth; marsupialisation considered for large cysts in growing patients.

terms.etiology_clue: Exposed necrotic bone persisting over eight weeks in patients receiving antiresorptive or antiangiogenic agents without prior radiation.
terms.management_clue: Drug holiday where possible, conservative debridement, antimicrobial rinses; surgical resection reserved for advanced refractory stage III disease.

terms.etiology_clue: Haematogenous spread of distant primary malignancy to jaw bones, most commonly from breast, lung, kidney, or prostate.
terms.management_clue: Palliative intent guides management; systemic oncologic therapy, radiotherapy for pain control, and surgical stabilisation if pathologic fracture present.

terms.etiology_clue: Hereditary haemoglobinopathy causing marrow hyperplasia, resulting in hair-on-end trabecular pattern and expansion of diploe on skull radiographs.
terms.management_clue: Medical management with transfusions and chelation therapy; oral manifestations managed supportively with specialist haematology co-management.

terms.etiology_clue: Inflammatory paradental cyst arising at the buccal furcation of a vital mandibular first molar in children, causing buccal bone expansion.
terms.management_clue: Enucleation with preservation of the associated vital tooth; prognosis is excellent with low recurrence rate.

terms.etiology_clue: Most common oral malignancy arising from surface epithelium, strongly associated with tobacco, alcohol, and high-risk HPV infection.
terms.management_clue: Surgical excision with clear margins, neck dissection for nodal disease, adjuvant radiotherapy or chemoradiation based on staging.

terms.etiology_clue: Benign fibro-osseous lesion of periodontal ligament origin occurring at apices of vital mandibular anterior teeth, predominantly in middle-aged Black women.
terms.management_clue: No treatment required; periodic radiographic monitoring sufficient as lesion is self-limiting and not associated with systemic disease.

terms.etiology_clue: Hamartomatous odontogenic malformation producing a disorganised calcified mass of enamel, dentine, and cementum without tooth-like morphology.
terms.management_clue: Conservative surgical excision; excellent prognosis with negligible recurrence risk and no malignant potential reported.

terms.etiology_clue: Sporadic GNAS somatic mutation causing replacement of normal bone with fibrous connective tissue producing a ground-glass radiographic appearance.
terms.management_clue: Conservative monitoring when stable; surgical recontouring for functional or aesthetic impairment; bisphosphonates for pain management in polyostotic disease.

terms.etiology_clue: Malignant neoplasm of melanocytes arising from oral mucosa, most commonly on the palate, presenting as pigmented irregular lesion with poor prognosis.
terms.management_clue: Wide surgical excision with adequate margins; adjuvant immunotherapy or targeted therapy; prognosis significantly worse than cutaneous melanoma.
